summary.intent: Fix serialization field count computation in RedactedConfig impls and get CI passing.
summary.outcome: Fixed dynamic field count serialization and resolved a rustfmt formatting violation in config.rs, then pushed both commits successfully.

repo: RedactedConfig has four Serialize impls that previously hardcoded max field counts; they now compute counts dynamically for non-JSON serializer compatibility.
The project uses cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt with strict formatting checks enforced in CI via a format-check recipe.
CI runs parallel local checks including biome, fmt, i18n, and zizmor.

code.finding: rustfmt requires serde_json::to_value chained call to be broken across two lines when it exceeds line length; single-line form was rejected by fmt check.

workflow: Local CI checks run in parallel; a single failure (fmt) causes the whole suite to report failure even if other checks pass.
zizmor is used for GitHub Actions security auditing and ran clean with 14 ignored and 47 suppressed findings.

friction: The fmt check failed after the initial push, requiring a second fix commit, because the formatter was not run locally before pushing.
Cached llama build directories were removed during CI, suggesting stale build artifacts occasionally interfere with the build.
